name: Ming Mang
author: AGP (traditional Tibetan; also Mig-mang, Gundru)
description: Ming Mang (Mig-mang; also called Gundru), the Tibetan custodial-CONVERSION game — not the Go variant that shares its name (see Tibetan Go). Each side starts with 2n-2 stones along two adjacent board edges and moves like a rook. Sandwich an unbroken enemy line between two of your stones and the whole line CONVERTS to your colour in place, Reversi-style — the board population never changes. Moving into a sandwich is safe; a move may not repeat any earlier position; a player who cannot move loses. Win by converting every enemy stone or blockading the opponent. Options: board size (8×8, 9×9 Gundru, 17×17 traditional) and the Rin-chen Lha-mo 1926 rule giving a player's LAST stone a draughts-style capture leap.
